title: Locus Story Contract
description: Machine-readable user story contract. A structured format for writing user stories that AI coding assistants, CI pipelines, and audit tools can read alongside humans.

$schema.description: Optional. URL to this JSON Schema, for editor tooling.

schema_version.description: Schema version. Semver string. Readers must check major version; handle unknown minor/patch gracefully.

exported_at.description: Optional. ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when this file was exported from the Locus platform. Present when the file was generated by the API or CLI export; absent when hand-authored.

stories.description: All stories for this project. May be empty.

Project.title: Project
Project.description: Project metadata. Stable across renames — use `id` as the canonical reference.

id.description: Stable UUID. Never changes. Optional in hand-authored files.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp. When the project was created. Optional in hand-authored files.

Story.title: Story
Story.description: A single user story. Describes what a user can do or see — implementation-agnostic, observable, testable.

id.description: Stable UUID. Never changes, even if title or story_id changes. Use this as the canonical reference in PRs, audit logs, and integrations. Optional in hand-authored files.

story_id.description: Human-readable ID, e.g. 'US-01'. May change if renumbered. Not stable — use `id` for persistent references.

title.description: Short, active-voice description of what the user can do. Should begin with '[Actor] can [action]' or similar.
examples: Employee can submit a leave request
Manager can approve or reject a leave request

description.description: Observable behaviour. What a user can do or see. Implementation-agnostic. Specific enough that a QA engineer can verify it without asking the author.

section.description: Feature area grouping for display and filtering. E.g. 'Auth', 'Dashboard', 'Leave Requests'.

status.description: Implementation status. Set by Locus's audit process or manually. Do not infer from other fields.

assignee.description: Username or email of the person responsible for implementing this story. Null if unassigned.

reviewer.description: Username or email of the person reviewing this story's implementation. Null if unassigned.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp. When the story was created.

updated_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp. When the story was last modified.

version.description: Monotonically increasing version counter. Starts at 1. Increments on every change.

pr_refs.description: GitHub PR URLs that implement this story. Optional. Strings only in files — rich PRRef objects are API-only. See design decision A.3.

jira_key.description: Linked Jira issue key. Null if no Jira link.

oneOf.description: Human-readable, testable conditions that define when this story is done. Not Gherkin/BDD — plain English. Each item should be verifiable by a QA engineer.
examples: Error message appears within 2s if email is invalid
Form state preserved if validation fails

acceptance_criteria.description: Testable acceptance conditions. Null or absent if not yet defined. Enables precise Story Guard scope checking and deterministic audit validation.

oneOf.description: List of story_id values that must be implemented before this story can be started.

depends_on.description: Story IDs that must be implemented before this story. Use story_id values (e.g. 'US-03'), not UUIDs. Null or absent if no dependencies.

oneOf.description: URL to the Figma frame, mockup, design token set, or other design artefact for this story.

design_ref.description: Link to the design reference for this story. Null if no design artefact exists.

oneOf.description: Explicit test file paths (optionally with line anchors) that verify this story. Makes audit deterministic — the audit checks these files rather than inferring from code search.

test_refs.description: Test file references for this story. Null or absent if not yet specified.

compliance.description: Compliance sign-off block. Include for stories requiring formal approval before implementation. Null or absent if not applicable.

oneOf.description: Freetext field for per-story rationale: design decisions, scope exclusions, ADR links, implementation constraints. Distinct from description (observable behaviour) and acceptance_criteria (testable contract).

notes.description: Author notes, rationale, or constraints. Not the observable behaviour (that's description). Null or absent if unused.

Compliance.title: Compliance
Compliance.description: Formal approval record for regulated-sector stories. Include when a human must sign off before implementation (PSD2/3, HIPAA, ISO 27001, SOC 2, or similar).

approved_by.description: Email or username of the approver (CTO, compliance officer, auditor).

approved_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when approval was granted.

oneOf.description: URL to the review issue, PR, or document where approval is recorded.

review_ref.description: Link to the approval record. Optional but recommended for audit trails.

oneOf.description: Free text reason for the compliance block, e.g. regulatory article reference.

audit_note.description: Explanation of why this story requires compliance sign-off. Optional.

$comment: Canonical URL: https://locus.dev/schema/v1.json — GitHub raw fallback: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jonybur-oc/prototyper-story-schema/main/schema/v1.1/stories.schema.json
